programmes.show_name: Super Surgeons: A Chance at Life
programmes.show_description: Ex-navy pilot Rich has a huge 16cm tumour running from his jaw to his heart. Meanwhile, nearly all the organs in Tracy's pelvis need removal to combat her stage four bowel cancer.
The story of the remarkable campaign to ban private handguns in Britain after the 1996 mass shooting at Dunblane Primary School, told using a bereaved dad's recently uncovered recordings.
South Brooklyn: Gordon helps a debt-laden Brooklyn restaurant with constantly clashing co-owners. Can he sort out their finances before it folds?
Desiree Burch joins Joe Lycett for some snow-filled shenanigans in Lapland, where they have a crack at ice fishing and get carried away on a sleigh ride.
Keith Brymer Jones and his partner Marj start ripping out rot at the chapel and pitch their plan to turn it into a home, pottery studio and events space to the local community.
Kirstie Allsopp shows us how to create beautiful crafts with items we already own. She transforms some fabric by dyeing it with turmeric, and embellishes an old lampshade.
Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with Amy and Takie, who wanted rid of renting in London, and Vicky and Andy who hoped to combine a home with two businesses in Wiltshire.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Neil Delamere in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
A Fine French Whine: Henri plans to take Kelly away from Woody by pretending that he's about to be deported and persuading Kelly to marry him so that he can stay in the US.
I'm Okay, You're Defective: Sam's horrified when, after six pregnancy-free months with Rebecca, she suggests they see a fertility specialist. Frasier and Lilith make wills.
Go Mate: On a quiet weekend away at a hotel, Rebecca and Sam ponder their parenthood plans. Meanwhile, back at the bar, the gang seek a way to beat the winter blues.
Mother's Day: Debra takes offence when Marie accuses her of being rude, and they end up not speaking to each other. Surely Ray won't make a bad situation worse, will he?
The Bigger Person: Ray and Frank realise they can exploit the fact that Debra and Marie aren't speaking to each other.
The First Time: When Debra accuses Ray of still not being able to pick up on her 'not in the mood' signals, they fondly recall the first night they spent together.
Odd Man Out: Frasier acts spontaneously when he realises that being single isn't all it's cracked up to be.
Frasier's Imaginary Friend: Frasier starts dating a model, but their busy schedules make it difficult for them to meet, leading his family to suspect that she isn't real.
The Gift Horse: As Martin's 65th birthday approaches, Frasier and Niles attempt to outdo each other with their gifts.
Halloween: Niles throws a fancy-dress Halloween party to raise money for the Library Association.
KISS and Sell: Will expert buyers Mike Wolfe and Frank Fritz want to rock and roll all night when they find masses of KISS merch in Nicky's barn? Plus: Mike goes for a test drive!
Channel 4's midday news summary.
Ford Escort RS2000: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend visit Dorset to fix up a Mk2 RS2000. It belongs to carpenter Mike, who gave up work to support his son who had a terrible accident.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Simon O'Brien and Max McMurdo head to Woking, where their finds include a sidecar to be transformed into a boat, and a water cylinder that Simon thinks could be a trendy lamp.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Neil Delamere in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Claudia Winkleman and Clare Balding present live coverage of Crufts 2026 from Birmingham. The Flyball gets off to a flying start. With Radzi Chinyanganya and Ellie Simmonds.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In France, renovations give Andrew and Richard sleepless nights. In Spain, things get a little too quiet for Abi and Darren, and Gordon and Shona's dream hangs in the balance.
The new run continues. The week's final hosts, Helen and Carl, have their work cut out to impress at their Nottinghamshire glampsite after ruffling feathers with their feedback all week.
The new run continues. The fourth host in South Wales, doctor Sophie, is usually vegan but is serving meat to try and win over her guests. Will it be just what the doctor ordered? (Ep4/5)
Lucy Alexander helps Tracy and Lee find a French holiday home in Lower Normandy, which they hope could one day become their permanent home, for their budget of £70k.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
Clare Balding presents and Claudia Winkleman reports as Crufts gathers pace with the judging of the Working and Pastoral Groups. Plus: athleticism and speed in the Agility and Flyball.
From a teen's suburban bedroom to the heart of Silicon Valley, the story of a heartbroken father's bid for the truth behind his daughter's death, caught up in a powerful algorithmic spiral.
On the Gogglebox: Britain's Got Talent, The Heat, 56 Days, Olivia Attwood: Getting Filthy Rich, Ed Stafford's Rite of Passage, and Alice Roberts: Our Hospital Through Time.
A man has broken his leg on Scafell Pike. As darkness closes in, can the mountain rescue team save him from potentially life-threatening plummeting temperatures? (Ep2/4)
Max's Bar & Grill: Gordon visits New Jersey to transform Max's Bar & Grill. It's been serving up famous hotdogs for almost 100 years but is close to collapse.
(2020) Comedy-drama with Michelle Pfeiffer. A skint Manhattan socialite moves to Paris with her son and a cat... which may or may not contain the spirit of her dead husband.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Dumfries, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien get to work on Maggie's farm, which boasts an extraordinary collection of memorabilia.
With planning permission granted, Keith and Marj start removing the pews, parquet floor and pulpit in the main hall, and make a discovery in the Sunday school ceiling vents.
It's the semi-final and the potters make mosaics and water fountains inspired by Rome's Trevi Fountain. Whose final-worthy creations will make a splash?
Joining the crew today is Neil Delamere. Speaking of Neils - can GB history-maker Neil Simpson build on the two medals he won at the last Winter Paralympics? The 2026 Games start today!
Frasier brings some of his patients into the bar, and Woody cuts Sam's hair. Another Sam - GB skier Cozens - will be cutting down the slopes at the Winter Paralympics, which start today!
Woody's stuck doing back-to-back shifts to pay for a ring for Kelly. And snowboarder Ollie Hill is hoping to make it back-to-back medals at his second Winter Paralympics, which start today!
Sam tries to get even with John Hill after breaking a tooth. Meanwhile with the Winter Paralympics starting today, what ups and downs are in store as GB hits the Italian hills and slopes?
Robert joins a strange support group. But it's ParalympicsGB who really need our support at the Winter Paralympics, which start today!
Everybody loves Raymond, including his and Debra's therapist. And everybody loves curling too. Can GB's wheelchair curlers make the podium in Italy? The Winter Paralympics start today!
Ray's unhappy with the kids' workload and complains to their teacher. Too bad they don't have actual music teacher - and GB snowboarder - Nina Sparks. The Winter Paralympics start today!
Frasier wants to know who the father of Roz's baby is. Well, despite becoming a dad last year, we know it's not GB snowboarder James Barnes-Miller... The Winter Paralympics start today!
Frasier gets caught up in the hype of his 1000th show. Meanwhile, the Winter Paralympics celebrates its 50th anniversary in Milano Cortina this year - and it all kicks off today!
Frasier agrees to go on a cruise ship to Alaska. Maybe he'd be better off cruising to Italy instead, then he'd have a front-row seat at the Winter Paralympics, which starts today!
Frasier begins a relationship with a dynamic criminal defence lawyer. While in Italy, GB's Neil Simpson aims to defend his Super-G skiing title at the Winter Paralympics, which start today!
Mike and Frank unearth a life-sized Elvis sculpture in rural Vermont. Meanwhile, Channel 4's All Shook Up with Winter Paralympics fever, ahead of the Games' opening ceremony tonight.
Channel 4's midday news summary.
Tim and Fuzz revamp a classic Swedish Saab. Sweden beat GB in the wheelchair curling at the last Winter Paralympics. Can GB get revenge in their clash at the 2026 Games, which start today?!

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Simon plans to make a dining table from an old seed drill. Will ParalympicsGB's well-drilled athletes make their mark on the medal table at the Winter Paralympics, which start today?
Neil Delamere joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Can GB's Mixed Team wheelchair curlers dent the hopes of favourites China at this year's Winter Paralympics, which start today?
The Flyball's wide open at the world-famous dog show. Meanwhile, will GB snowboarder Davy Zyw be top dog at the Winter Paralympics, which start today? There's no ball, but he'll be flying.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: Andrew and Richard are chasing their dream lives in Europe. And do you know who else is chasing their dreams in Europe? That's right, ParalympicsGB. The Winter Paralympics start today!
All the hosts are unhappy with their shocking scores. They're going to wish they were more like ParalympicsGB, who begin their bid to bring home some Winter Paralympics wins today!
In South Wales, Sandra's going heavy on cream and mushrooms. But which athletes will be the crème de la crème of the Winter Paralympics? Which, in case you hadn't guessed, begins today!
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
...Ceremony: Ade Adepitan, Ed Jackson, Dame Sarah Storey and Alice Tai present live as the Milano Cortina Paralympics begin. Which athletes are the ones to watch over the next nine days?
The Goggleboxers settle down for this week's biggest and best TV.
Comedians Nick Mohammed and Vittorio Angelone and former Conservative politician Penny Mordaunt dissect the week's news with Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker.

programmes.show_name: Rhod Gilbert: The Book of John
programmes.show_description: Raw, personal and brutally honest, award-winning comedian Rhod takes us on a no-nonsense journey into finding humour when you've hit rock-bottom. Recorded at the Wales Millennium Centre.
